decisions.rationale: Risk of breaking existing marco* resources if deployed prematurely.
decisions.title: IaC: finish files only, no deploy yet
decisions.decision: Do not deploy to Azure now. Finish IaC files (Bicep + bootstrap.sh) in EsDAICoE-Sandbox / marco* resources first. Deploy when integration tests are ready.

decisions.rationale: ACA must be usable by any paying client. Tying auth to EsDAICoE org would prevent commercial use.
decisions.title: Auth: standalone multi-tenant Microsoft Entra (authority=common)
decisions.decision: ACA is standalone private-sector SaaS. NOT tied to EsDAICoE org. Multi-tenant Microsoft Entra (authority=common). Any client Microsoft account works. What matters: delegated token has Reader + Cost Management Reader on CLIENT subscription.

decisions.rationale: bootstrap.sh was not previously specified. Needs full authoring.
decisions.title: bootstrap.sh: new file from scratch (not update)
decisions.decision: infra/phase1-marco/bootstrap.sh is a new file. Must cover: Cosmos containers, KV secrets, ACA Container App + 3 Jobs, APIM product. DO_CONTAINERAPPS and DO_APIM toggle flags required.

decisions.rationale: Prices are business decisions, not engineering constants. Coupons give sales flexibility without code changes.
decisions.title: Stripe: env-var prices, promotion codes enabled, full coupon waiver supported
decisions.decision: Prices NOT hardcoded. STRIPE_PRICE_* are env vars. Add STRIPE_COUPON_ENABLED: bool. Pass allow_promotion_codes=True to checkout sessions. Full fee waiver via coupon is supported for trials/partnerships.

decisions.rationale: Monthly subscription provides predictable MRR. One-time for Tier 3 matches the per-engagement delivery model.
decisions.title: Stripe: Tier 2 = monthly subscription (CAD), Tier 3 = one-time
decisions.decision: Tier 2 uses mode=subscription with monthly recurring billing in CAD. Tier 3 uses mode=one_time per engagement.

decisions.rationale: Keeps Phase 1 simpler. Marco* sandbox is already Bicep-provisioned. Terraform adds complexity without Phase 1 value.
decisions.title: Delivery templates: Bicep only for Phase 1 (no Terraform)
decisions.decision: 12 delivery template folders contain main.bicep + README.md only. No main.tf in Phase 1. Generator skips missing files gracefully (TemplateNotFound pass). Terraform deferred to Phase 2 / Epic 11.

decisions.rationale: Simple is sufficient for Phase 1. Complex parametrization can be added in Phase 2.
decisions.title: Delivery templates: simple Jinja2 variable substitution
decisions.decision: Templates use {{ scan_id }}, {{ subscription_id }}, and finding-specific fields. No complex logic. Jinja2 rendering already in generator.py.

decisions.rationale: 95% is achievable with fixture-based unit tests. Playwright is preferred over Cypress for headless CI.
decisions.title: Test coverage: 95% end-to-end target, Playwright headless, hardcoded fixtures
decisions.decision: 95% line coverage on all analysis rule modules. Unit tests use hardcoded JSON fixtures (no Cosmos calls). Playwright headless for E2E a11y. All 5 locales exercised in Playwright suite.

decisions.rationale: Playwright is lighter than Cypress for headless CI, already supported by @axe-core/playwright.
decisions.title: a11y testing: Playwright headless in GitHub Actions CI
decisions.decision: Playwright headless (not Cypress). Runs on ubuntu-latest in GitHub Actions. axe-core assertions injected into Playwright tests. Zero critical/serious violations gates PR merge.

decisions.rationale: Shipping all 5 locales in Phase 1 maximizes market coverage from day 1 without blocking on professional translators.
decisions.title: i18n: all 5 locales ship in Phase 1 (best-effort machine translation)
decisions.decision: en, fr, es, de, pt-BR all live in Phase 1. fr-CA is reviewed before launch. pt-BR, es, de are best-effort machine translation. Professional review for non-EN/FR locales is Phase 2 hardening.

decisions.rationale: Custom domain requires DNS delegation, TLS setup, and a purchased domain. Not needed for Phase 1 dogfood.
decisions.title: Phase 1 deployment: Azure Container Apps free hostnames, no custom domain
decisions.decision: Phase 1 uses *.{region}.azurecontainerapps.io free hostnames. No custom domain required. PUBLIC_APP_URL and PUBLIC_API_URL read from env vars. Phase 2 gets real custom domain (TBD).

decisions.rationale: Moving org adds friction with no Phase 1 benefit. Private repo in eva-foundry is sufficient for controlled beta.
decisions.title: Repository: stays in eva-foundry org (private), deployed to Azure
decisions.decision: Repo 51-ACA stays in eva-foundry GitHub org (private). No org move needed for Phase 1. Deployed to Azure subscription EsDAICoE-Sandbox. Commercial org move can be evaluated at Phase 2.
